$comment: Source of truth for the apodictic:warrant_plan block (Nonfiction Pre-Draft Pathway, Increment 3 — the warrant pre-check). One block per subclaim, planning the WARRANT (the principle that connects the support to the claim) the argument will rely on. Links a spine subclaim (subclaim_id, a Cn in the argument_spine ladder) and SEEDS Argument_State §4 Warrant and Inference Map (docs/argument-state-schema.md): warrant_status EXPLICIT/RECOVERABLE/MISSING/CONTESTED; backing PRESENT/THIN/ABSENT; qualifier MATCHED/OVERCONFIDENT/UNDERCLAIMED. The point of pre-checking warrants: for a HOSTILE audience an implicit (non-EXPLICIT) or unbacked (ABSENT) warrant must be made explicit and backed before drafting (validator W3, audience-calibrated against the spine's audience_receptivity). The Firewall holds: the writer plans the connecting principle; the engine does not invent the argument. Validated by scripts/argument_spine.py (A7/A8/A9/W3). See docs/nonfiction-pre-draft.md.
title: APODICTIC warrant plan (apodictic.warrant_plan.v1)
